meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn how to implement a high-velocity client workflow to identify profitable SaaS projects using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'ideas' and start mining for 'problems.' By implementing a systematic client workflow, you identify high-friction manual processes within specific industries. Use Antigravity to model the data architecture and Claude Code to rapidly prototype the automation layer, turning client pain points into a monetizable SaaS MVP.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’re spinning cycles waiting for a lightning-bolt idea while your competitors are already shipping value. The reality is that profitable SaaS products aren't 'invented' in a vacuum; they are discovered at the intersection of recurring client pain and inefficient manual workflows.

This guide moves you from the paralysis of ideation to the clarity of execution. By adopting a structured client-first workflow, you’ll stop building features nobody wants and start architecting solutions that businesses are desperate to pay for. We’re leveraging Antigravity for rapid domain modeling and Claude Code to accelerate your development velocity.

steps.title: Identify the High-Friction Workflow
steps.description: Select a niche where you have existing contacts. Interview them specifically about manual data entry or repetitive tasks they perform in spreadsheets. Document the exact sequence of their current workflow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following client workflow: [Paste Notes]. Create a schema for a database that tracks these entities and their relationships, optimized for automation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README outlining the problem, target audience, and the proposed technical architecture.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Logic
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define the core logic of the workflow. This ensures your MVP isn't just a UI shell, but a functional engine that solves the underlying data bottleneck.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the business logic layer for a CRUD interface that manages the client workflow identified in step 1, ensuring data integrity across state transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the backend service file based on the Antigravity schema.'

steps.title: Rapid MVP Prototyping
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the boilerplate and core functional modules. Focus on the 'Happy Path' of the workflow to ensure the core value proposition is viable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a scaffold for a React frontend that consumes the API endpoints defined in the previous logic layer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scaffold the frontend project structure and implement the primary interaction component.'

steps.title: Implement Workflow Automation
steps.description: Connect the data inputs to an automation trigger. This is where the manual process becomes an AI-assisted workflow, drastically reducing user time-to-value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a webhook handler that processes incoming client data and triggers the core workflow logic automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the webhook handler and integrate a mock AI service to process the incoming payload.'

steps.title: Validation and Deployment
steps.description: Push to a staging environment and get the tool into the hands of your test client. Measure the 'Time Saved' metric to validate the business case.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a testing script that validates the workflow completion time against manual benchmarks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the CI/CD pipeline for deployment to Vercel or your preferred cloud provider.'

pro_tips: Don't build a dashboard; build a workflow. Clients pay for results, not for pretty charts.
Target 'Spreadsheet Hell'—if a client is manually copying data between tabs, you have a million-dollar SaaS opportunity.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests first. It forces you to define the expected workflow outcome before writing a single line of implementation code.
Charge a setup fee for the initial integration. It separates the tire-kickers from the serious business partners.
Optimize for 'Time to First Value.' If the user doesn't see a benefit within 180 seconds, your workflow is too complex.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, conduct a 'Day-in-the-Life' review with your first client. Observe them using the tool, identify friction points in your UI, and iterate your next sprint based on their actual behavior, not their feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the roadmap to turn a client's frustration into a subscription-based product. Stop waiting for inspiration and start building the bridge to your first revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
